Global (All).title: Rise in global cyber attacks
Global (All).summary: A recent surge in global cyber attacks has been identified, with multiple high-profile targets affected.

Global (All).title: Escalating tensions between global superpowers
Global (All).summary: Tensions between major global superpowers have increased in the past week, sparking concerns about potential conflict.

Global (All).title: Growing number of protests and civil unrest globally
Global (All).summary: Protests and civil unrest have been reported in multiple countries around the world, with demonstrators taking to the streets to express their discontent.

United States.title: Tension Escalation Over US-China Trade Dispute
United States.summary: The US and China remain at odds over trade agreements, with the US imposing additional tariffs on Chinese goods.

United States.title: Federal Judge Temporarily Blocks Abortion Ban in Wyoming
United States.summary: A federal judge has temporarily blocked a ban on abortions in Wyoming, following a lawsuit filed by the American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU).

United States.title: Biden Administration Announces Plan to Address Infant Formula Shortage
United States.summary: The Biden administration has announced a plan to address the infant formula shortage in the US, including increased imports and emergency shipments.

South Korea.title: Anti-govt protesters clash with police in Seoul over labour reform plans
South Korea.summary: Labor unions in South Korea plan to hold a massive rally in Seoul on Monday to protest the government's new labor reform plans, which they claim will lead to unfair dismissal.

South Korea.title: Yoon administration says it will take 'all necessary steps' to strengthen security
South Korea.summary: South Korean President Yoon Suk-yeol vowed to take "all necessary steps" to strengthen the country's security in the face of North Korea's increasing nuclear and missile threats.

South Korea.title: South Korean, US navies hold exercises in Yellow Sea amid tensions with North
South Korea.summary: The South Korean and US navies conducted joint exercises in the Yellow Sea on Tuesday, a move likely aimed at deterring North Korea's recent military activities.

Japan.title: Japan's Fossil Fuel Policy Sparks Protests
Japan.summary: Thousands of protesters have taken to the streets of Tokyo and other Japanese cities to voice their opposition to the government's plan to boost fossil fuel production, citing environmental concerns and climate change risks.

Japan.title: Japan's Ruling Party Loses Majority, Risks Election Call
Japan.summary: A surprise loss for Japan's ruling party in a parliamentary election has raised the prospect of a snap election, potentially destabilizing the country's politics and economic policies.

Japan.title: Japanese PM's Resignation Sparks Bidding War for Leadership
Japan.summary: The resignation of Japanese Prime Minister Fumio Kishida has sparked a flurry of activity among potential successors, with several top politicians vying for the position and promising different policy agendas.
